title: pleopeltis polypodioides var. polypodioides
intro: Pleopeltis polypodioides var. polypodioides, also known as the resurrection fern, is a species of epiphytic fern that has adapted to survive in dry environments by curling up its leaves to conserve water. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Pleopeltis polypodioides var. polypodioides include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with some indigenous communities also using it in traditional remedies.
